file_insights.purpose: QEMU VM adapter for executing bop cards in isolated VMs running zam unikernel
key_functions: main: entry point accepting workdir, prompt_file, stdout_log, stderr_log, optional memory_out
run_tests: self-test suite with 5 test cases covering path resolution, zam candidate list, QEMU command construction, and rate-limit detection
critical_operations: Resolves relative paths to absolute before QEMU invocation
Searches for zam image across 4 candidate paths (release/debug builds and .elf variants)
Mounts card bundle via 9P virtfs with mount_tag=card for VM access
Redirects UART output to stdout_log via -serial file: redirection
Detects rate-limiting via exit code 75 or stderr content matching (429, rate limit, too many requests)

exit_code_handling.75: transient rate-limit - card returns to pending/ for retry

patterns_discovered.name: path_resolution_pattern
patterns_discovered.description: Conditional absolute-to-relative path conversion before subprocess invocation
patterns_discovered.implementation: if path starts-with '/' use as-is, else join with pwd
patterns_discovered.rationale: Enables adapter to work with both relative (from dispatcher) and absolute paths without breaking QEMU 9P mount references

patterns_discovered.name: graceful_dependency_failure
patterns_discovered.description: Multi-line error message with candidate paths, build instructions, and architectural explanation when zam image is missing
patterns_discovered.implementation: Check 4 candidate paths, provide helpful error when none exist
patterns_discovered.rationale: Prevents silent failures and educates user on project architecture (zam is separate external dependency)

patterns_discovered.name: exit_code_remapping
patterns_discovered.description: zam exit code 75 (rate-limit) is preserved; other non-zero codes trigger failure path
patterns_discovered.implementation: Capture QEMU exit_code via | complete, check stderr for rate-limit indicators, exit with detected code
patterns_discovered.rationale: Allows dispatcher to distinguish transient (retry-worthy) from permanent failures

patterns_discovered.name: embedded_self_test_suite
patterns_discovered.description: 5 integrated tests run with --test flag covering path logic, candidate list construction, command building, and stderr parsing
patterns_discovered.rationale: Enables quick validation without external test harness; follows bop project pattern of embedding unit tests in adapters

patterns_discovered.name: QEMU_command_construction
patterns_discovered.description: Multi-flag QEMU invocation with kernel image, 9P virtfs mount, serial redirection, memory, and boot options
patterns_discovered.rationale: Provides isolated VM execution, filesystem access via 9P, and structured output capture for bop card processing

gotchas_discovered.impact: Adapter will fail until zam is built; no runtime discovery mechanism
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Comments indicate 'Path will change once zam build system is finalized'; currently requires external build

gotchas_discovered.issue: Rate-limit detection logic may miss edge cases
gotchas_discovered.context: Checks stderr for '429', 'rate limit', 'too many requests' (case-insensitive) OR exit code 75
gotchas_discovered.impact: If rate-limiting wrapper emits different error text, card may incorrectly route to failed/ instead of pending/
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: zam should ideally always exit with code 75 for rate-limit; stderr check is defensive backup

gotchas_discovered.issue: No timeout mechanism for long-running VMs
gotchas_discovered.context: QEMU invocation has no -rtc, -icount, or external timeout wrapper
gotchas_discovered.impact: Hung zam unikernel could block dispatcher indefinitely
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Likely handled by dispatcher wrapper or timeout_wrapper.nu; adapter assumes external timeout contract

gotchas_discovered.issue: Path resolution must occur before any directory changes
gotchas_discovered.context: Code resolves paths upfront and stores in _abs variables
gotchas_discovered.impact: Critical correctness: if paths were resolved after cd or in subprocess, would fail or reference wrong mount
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Implementation correctly handles this; documented via comment

gotchas_discovered.impact: May be insufficient for large cardprompts or tool invocations; over-provisioned for minimal unikernels
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Reasonable default for zam (<5MB target); can parameterize in Wave 5 if needed

approach_outcome.description: Create adapters/qemu.nu with VM execution logic
approach_outcome.test_coverage: 5 embedded unit tests (path resolution, candidate list, command construction, rate-limit detection)

recommendations.recommendation: Document zam build process in project README or CONTRIBUTING.md
recommendations.rationale: Adapter fails gracefully but users need clear instructions to bootstrap zam; currently must know to build separately

recommendations.recommendation: Cache zam image path discovery result in environment variable
recommendations.rationale: Current implementation searches 4 paths on every card execution; one-time discovery per dispatcher session would reduce overhead

recommendations.recommendation: Add verbose/debug mode logging for QEMU command construction and exit code details
recommendations.rationale: Helps troubleshoot VM boot failures, 9P mount issues, and rate-limit edge cases without modifying code

recommendations.recommendation: Plan Wave 5 enhancements: dub EFI boot (replace -kernel), configurable memory allocation, timeout wrapper integration
recommendations.rationale: Current implementation is Wave 1 stepping stone; comments indicate architectural roadmap for hardened boot chain and flexible resource tuning

recommendations.recommendation: Add integration test that invokes qemu.nu with mock zam image on test VM
recommendations.rationale: Current tests only validate path and command construction logic; end-to-end QEMU invocation untested (requires zam availability)
